title: 60 bullets in 2mins: Plush Gurgaon society turns battlefield; class 11 student among 4 dead
content: Four hired hitmen died and one was critically injured during a police encounter. The shooters were sent by gangster Deepak Nandal for extortion. Three police officers sustained bullet injuries in the fierce gunfight. The encounter occurred after the hitmen fired at a businessman's house. This incident marks a significant escalation in extortion crimes in Delhi-NCR.

title: What drove BJP to pull its Bankipur nominee days before Prashant Kishor face-off
content: BJP's Abhishek Kumar Sinha withdrew from the Bankipur bypoll citing family reasons. The party then fielded Neeraj Kumar Sinha as its new candidate for the contest. Opposition parties claimed the BJP was nervous about a potential defeat in the upcoming election. Jan Suraaj founder Prashant Kishor is campaigning against the ruling party. Polling for the Bankipur bypoll is scheduled for July 30.

title: Indian cricket set to undergo a coaching reset?
content: At least one prominent support staff member is likely to leave the Team India camp despite having an extension clause in the contract. The possible exits, unrelated to the recent T20I defeats, come amid dressing-room unease and growing differences between the coaching staff and selectors.

title: Murder rampage: Man out on bail kills Pocso victim, her family & then his own; manhunt on
content: A farmer allegedly murdered six people in Shabad and his village. He killed a teenage girl, her mother, and grandmother after a POCSO complaint. The accused then murdered his wife and two young sons at their home. He drove to a lake and killed the girl before returning home. Police have launched a manhunt for the absconding accused.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai shares thanks note for Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez’s fund
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai thanked partners for launching new wildfire detection satellites.  These advanced FireSat satellites can spot fires invisible to existing technology.  The expanded satellite network builds upon a successful pilot program launched last year.  The Bezos Earth Fund provided a significant new investment to support this vital project.  This initiative aims to provide near real-time wildfire updates globally.

title: Mikel Merino creates FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player to...
content: Mikel Merino scored another decisive late goal for Spain against Belgium. This victory propelled La Roja into the FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinals. Merino's impactful substitute appearances have now made World Cup history. Spain will face France in their next crucial tournament match. The team's unbeaten run in regular time now extends significantly.

title: 'Stone pelting, highway blockade': Protest erupts after BJP denies leader bypoll ticket
content: Supporters of Narottam Mishra protested the BJP's decision to deny him a ticket. They blocked the Jhansi-Gwalior highway, demanding the party reconsider its candidate choice. This action caused significant traffic disruptions for several hours on Friday evening. The BJP had announced Ashutosh Tiwari as their candidate for the Datia by-election. Local party office bearers also submitted resignations in solidarity with Mishra.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use. He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption. The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings. Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave. This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: Akshay Kumar details sleep, workout and diet; offers 8-8-8 rule guide
content: Akshay Kumar exemplifies peak fitness, adhering to strict routines and a nutritious diet. He emphasizes the importance of seven to eight hours of sleep along with an early start every day. His fitness activities span martial arts, yoga, and bodyweight training for enhanced agility. Adopting a vegan diet and practicing weekly fasting significantly boost his overall wellness.

title: Milky Way's coldest objects may not be stars but alien-built Dyson swarms: Study
content: Recent studies indicate that certain chill entities scattered across our galaxy might actually be artificial constructs created by extraterrestrial life forms. These colossal structures could function as energy collection systems established by intelligent civilizations. Researchers are on the lookout for distinctive thermal emissions and faint visible light from these objects, leveraging the capabilities of the James Webb Space Telescope and Project Hephaistos.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Digital slate & classroom exchange: Questions cloud Jaipur schoolgirl death
content: Amaira Meena's family claims the police chargesheet is incomplete regarding her death.  Newly surfaced footage raises questions about institutional accountability and child safety.  The family seeks additional charges against the class teacher and school management.  Parents question omissions of school principal and founder from the chargesheet.  A parents' body also demands an investigation into school irregularities.

title: Meta drops AI image feature on Instagram after outcry over privacy concerns
content: Meta has withdrawn a controversial AI image generation tool. This feature allowed users to create images using public Instagram photos. Privacy advocates and Hollywood agencies strongly protested the tool's design. The company admitted the feature missed public sentiment and is now unavailable. Meta plans to introduce other generative AI features across its platforms.

title: Ethanol scam: Subsidised rice being diverted to pvt mills in MP, probe ordered
content: Police formed an SIT to investigate alleged diversion of subsidised rice in Madhya Pradesh.  Consignments meant for ethanol plants were reportedly rerouted to private mills.  This rice was then reintroduced into the procurement and milling cycle.  Four individuals have been arrested as the investigation into the suspected racket continues.  Authorities are committed to uncovering the full extent of this exploitation.

title: Who are Lamine Yamal's parents? Meet Mounir Nasraoui and Sheila Ebana
content: Lamine Yamal's parents, Mounir Nasraoui from Morocco and Sheila Ebana from Equatorial Guinea, both immigrated to Spain and raised Yamal in the greater Barcelona area under modest financial conditions. After separating when Yamal was around three years old, his mother raised him in Rocafonda, Mataró, while working to make ends meet. His name honors two men who helped his family during a period of hardship. Despite his Spanish nationality and international football career with La Roja, Yamal proudly represents his Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean heritage, wearing his parents' national flags on his boots at every match.

title: Stefon Diggs case: Who is accuser Christopher Blake Griffith?
content: Stefon Diggs, the former New England Patriots’ wide receiver, has been dealing with many challenging things in his life for the past few months, ever since he was released by the team after losing in the Super Bowl, a few months ago. While his high profile break up with Cardi B had sparked chaos, the controversial assault allegation made by a man named Christopher Blake Griffith has shocked millions.

title: Blackhawks, Jets eye $38.5M Canucks winger in latest trade buzz
content: The Vancouver Canucks’ winger Jake DeBrusk has been a regular name concerning trade speculations, ahead of the new season. As the team seeks a roster rebuild, a number of teams have been linked to him. Amid all the mess, the Chicago Blackhawks and the Winnipeg Jets have emerged as potential landing spots.

title: Roadmap to 2030: India, New Zealand seal 18 pacts across defence, trade and security
content: India and New Zealand elevated their ties to a strategic partnership. Prime Ministers Modi and Luxon signed eighteen agreements for cooperation. This roadmap guides future collaboration across multiple key sectors. Bilateral trade aims to reach seven billion New Zealand dollars by 2030. Both nations will enhance maritime security and disaster management efforts.

title: 'If this happiness improves my father's health ... ': Prabhsimran on India call-up
content: Prabhsimran Singh has finally earned his maiden India call-up after back-to-back impressive IPL seasons with Punjab Kings. In this exclusive interview, the opener recalls his father's emotional reaction, explains how consistency and converting starts into big scores changed his career, and reflects on the role of Shreyas Iyer, Ricky Ponting and Yuvraj Singh in shaping his journey to the Indian team.

title: Newlyweds Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt to honeymoon in Australia?
content: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are enjoying a family holiday in Melbourne. They are accompanied by their sons, Quinn and Azad, for this trip. Aamir will attend the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne next year. He has received a Best Actor nomination for his role in 'Sitaare Zameen Par'. The festival will feature various film-related events and award ceremonies.

title: Shubman Gill chats with Federer, becomes only fourth Indian captain to...
content: Shubman Gill received a Royal Box invitation to Wimbledon 2026. He joined elite Indian captains previously hosted in the prestigious enclosure. Gill also met tennis legend Roger Federer and watched matches with Sachin Tendulkar. Earlier, he attended the British Grand Prix as a VIP guest. The Indian captain is currently in England for an ODI series.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' box office collection Day 1: Film beats estimates with Rs 21.5cr worldwide
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Dhamaal 4' made a remarkable debut, surpassing early box office forecasts. With an impressive opening day collection of approximately Rs 13.75 crore net, it emerged as the leading new release amidst fierce competition. The film enjoyed robust audience attendance and steady foot traffic. Experts predict a significant uptick in earnings as the weekend approaches.

title: Man bitten by rattlesnake in California required 54 vials of antivenom to survive
content: An Idaho man required an exceptional fifty-four vials of antivenom after a rattlesnake bite.  His venom spread rapidly, causing severe symptoms and a dangerous blood clotting disorder.  Oroville Hospital exhausted its antivenom supply before transferring him.  Doctors administered additional doses at Stanford Hospital to stabilize his condition.  He is still recovering at home after a lengthy hospital stay.

title: Vulnerable but valuable: Why US uses MQ‑9 Reapers; India set to get 31 drones
content: US Central Command has lost nearly thirty MQ-9 Reaper drones to Iranian forces. These drones have been vital for surveillance and strike missions in West Asia. India is acquiring over thirty Reapers for its army, air force, and navy. The procurement includes local assembly and maintenance facilities for the drones. Deliveries of these advanced aircraft are scheduled to commence in 2029.

title: Sadhguru explains how rudraksha protects your energy
content: Rudraksha beads hold deep significance in Indian spiritual traditions and practices.  Sadhguru explains rudraksha enhances a person's energy field and aura.  It traditionally served as a protective shield for travelers and mendicants.  Ancient beliefs suggest rudraksha could detect poison in water sources.  Traditional views also link rudraksha to calming effects and health benefits.

title: 'Make a public statement or ...': US presses Iran to guarantee Strait of Hormuz is fully open
content: Washington is pressuring Tehran for a public statement ensuring Strait of Hormuz shipping safety.  United States officials warned of negative consequences if Iran refuses this demand.  Iran has attributed recent shipping incidents to an errant system part.  President Trump declared a six-day ceasefire over due to alleged violations.  Fifteen ships transited the strait recently, with many more waiting.

title: Monsoon session: NDA in command, opposition in disarray. Will DMK tilt the balance?
content: The opposition parties are grappling with significant turmoil following vital splits that have diminished their strength in Parliament. Recent shifts have bolstered the ruling NDA coalition's standing. As the Monsoon session approaches, the potential reintroduction of a Constitution Amendment Bill looms. Internal fractures within several regional parties could further compromise the opposition's capacity to impede crucial legislation.

title: Belgium end Unai Simon's record 650-minute shutout streak; Spain reach World Cup semis
content: Spain advanced to the World Cup semifinals after a 2-1 quarterfinal victory over Belgium. Goalkeeper Unai Simon's record 650-minute shutout streak concluded when Charles De Ketelaere scored. Mikel Merino secured Spain's win with a late goal, his second crucial strike in the tournament. Belgium's hopes were dashed after their goalkeeper sustained an injury during the match. This victory extended Spain's impressive unbeaten run to thirty-seven games.

title: FIFA World Cup: Merino magic helps Spain edge Belgium, set up France semifinal
content: Mikel Merino scored a late winner to secure Spain's World Cup semi-final spot. Spain defeated Belgium 2-1 in a thrilling quarter-final match on Friday. They will now face tournament favorites France in their next game. Spain extended their impressive unbeaten run to thirty-seven matches. Belgium's eighteen-game undefeated streak concluded with this loss.

title: Pak refuses to accept grooming gang leader unless UK hands over political dissidents
content: Pakistan demands UK extradition of dissidents for deporting Shabir Ahmed.  Ahmed, a grooming gang leader, faces deportation after his British citizenship was revoked.  Pakistan states it will not accept him without reciprocal extradition agreements.  Islamabad wants UK extradition of figures like Shahzad Akbar and Adil Raja.  This creates a stalemate, with the UK unlikely to extradite dissidents.

title: IND vs ENG: Away from abject T20I showing, India begin preps for ODI series today
content: Members of India's ODI squad, including Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma, and Shubman Gill, are likely to begin practicing at Edgbaston for the three-match series. Over 200 kilometres away, the T20I setup will look to avoid yet another 'first'.

title: Ram temple trust open to selecting woman CEO, new pick within 30 days
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is seeking a new Chief Executive Officer. A three-member committee will complete the selection process within the next thirty days. Honesty and integrity are foremost criteria for the potential Chief Executive Officer. The chosen individual must foster harmony and command respect from all stakeholders. This appointment will ensure smooth pilgrim facilities and administrative functions.

title: FIFA puts World Cup final pitch up for sale at Rs. 42,989 per piece
content: FIFA is marketing pieces of World Cup final turf for USD 450, but the initiative has sparked outrage from both fans and players. The turf's poor quality has come under fire for its irregular texture. Additionally, the ticket prices for the tournament have skyrocketed, creating alarm among sports enthusiasts. Notably, shipping is limited to customers in the United States and Europe.

title: Andy Burnham poised to be next UK PM
content: Andy Burnham is expected to become Labour leader next week.  He will then take office as Britain’s new prime minister on July 20.  Burnham secured significant backing from Labour MPs for his leadership bid.  He will replace Keir Starmer, who is expected to resign that day.  Burnham aims to offer a new political approach for the country.

title: Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding fuels Blake Lively fallout claims
content: A new report claims Taylor Swift's friendship fallout with Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds has also affected her relationship with their four children, to whom she is reportedly a godmother. The claims resurfaced after the family's absence from Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding, although another report said Swift remains close to her other godson, Leo Thames, despite reduced contact with his mother, Jaime King.

title: PM Modi praises Australia social ban for U-16s, hinting at similar curbs
content: India is considering stricter age limits for social media access. Prime Minister Modi praised Australia's ban on platforms for children under sixteen. This move follows growing government focus on online safety for minors. Several Indian states have also proposed similar restrictions on social media use. Nationwide implementation faces significant challenges due to India's vast internet user population.

title: 'You can't afford to ... ': 1983 World Cup winner warns Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: 1983 World Cup winner Madan Lal urged patience with 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after his struggles in India's T20I series loss to England. He said the youngster must learn to build an innings, adding, "You can't afford to hit every ball." Madan Lal also criticised India's overall performance, saying rebuilding a strong team takes time while success can be quickly undone.

title: Spain equal Argentina's record with 36-game unbeaten run after late Belgium win
content: Mikel Merino's late goal secured Spain's World Cup semifinal place. This victory also matched Argentina's record for the second-longest unbeaten run. Spain now holds a 36-game streak without defeat in international football. They are now one match away from Italy's all-time unbeaten record. Spain will face France in their next crucial semifinal match.

title: INS Mahendragiri: All you need to know about the Indian Navy’s new indigenous stealth frigate
content: The frigate takes its name from the Mahendragiri mountain range in the Eastern Ghats, symbolising strength, resilience and determination

title: INS Mahendragiri set to be commissioned at Visakhapatnam’s Eastern Naval Command
content: The commissioning ceremony is on Saturday being presided over by Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, who arrived in the city on July 10—coinciding with his 75th birthday—for a two-day visit.

title: PM Modi in New Zealand LIVE: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi landed in Auckland last night in the third and final leg of his three-nation tour that largely focused on expanding cooperation in the Indo-Pacific against the backdrop of China’s increasing assertiveness in the region

title: How two ‘objectionable’ books triggered a crackdown on J&K’s varsity and college libraries | Explained
content: Around 251 copies of these books were already circulated among the schools in J&K. The J&K administration said the introduction of these books was a result of  “serious negligence, dereliction of duty and lack of proper due diligence”, and blamed the members of the Sub Committee, which approved it.

title: Govt. amends rules to strengthen regulation of drug formulations with high alcohol content
content: High alcohol-containing formulations to require licences and to be dispensed only under prescription under Schedule H1 of the Drugs Rules, 1945

title: Meerut student's death: Police used excessive force against protesters, says Chandrashekhar Azad
content: A large number of Azad Samaj Party supporters also gathered at the toll plaza, leading to a tense situation in the area for several hours

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cloudy skies to persist in Delhi; highways blocked after landslides in hill States
content: Widespread rainfall lashed large parts of Uttar Pradesh on July 10, bringing daytime temperatures below normal at several places, with heavy to very heavy rainfall being forecast at isolated places

title: Bihar’s per capita power consumption increased more than five times to 401 units from 75 in 2005
content: Once known as a power-starved state, Bihar witnessed a record power supply of 9426 MW from a paltry supply of 700 MW in 2005; government initiatives improved power supply infrastructure over the years

title: Bihar BJP candidate withdraws his candidature from Bankipur bypoll
content: Party insiders says Abhishek Kumar alias Bunty was considered a weak candidate against Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor; candidate withdraws citing family reasons

title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; target ₹35,000 crore trade by 2030
content: A joint statement noted that Narendra Modi and Christopher Luxon exchanged views on the Indo-Pacific, reaffirming their shared commitment to a free, open, and prosperous region

title: Bihar CM transfers ₹1,423.94 crore to 97.84 lakh social security pensioners
content: He also announced that 10th of every month will be observed as ‘Bihar Pension Day’ when pension amounts will be transferred to the accounts of all eligible social security pensioners via DBT.

title: Madras High Court restrains Election Commission from notifying byelections in five constituencies in Tamil Nadu
content: Chief Justices Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan issue the interim injunction till July 31, 2026, with respect to Tiruchi East, Perundurai, Ambasamudram, Viralimalai, and Karur constituencies.

title: E20 fuel may lead to 3-5% mileage hit in ‘some’ vehicles, Govt. admits as it defends blending
content: However, the government added that E20 fuel has a higher-octane rating, and provides several other vehicular and emission-related advantages over unblended fuel

title: Railways issue alert after cable cuts trigger signal failures, safety concerns
content: ‘Repeated signal failures pose serious threat to the safety of train services, and can lead to catastrophic consequences if precautions are not taken’

title: Datia bypoll: BJP denies ticket to Narottam Mishra, fields Ashutosh Tiwari
content: Byelection in Datia was announced by the Election Commission of India on July 7, for which polling will be held on July 30 and counting of votes will take place on August 3. The last date to file nomination is July 13.

title: IITs, IIMs push back against VBSA norms
content: Institutes want guarantees on autonomy, clarity on applicability of statutory provisions governing them; “differential and unintended” consequences of penalty system also flagged

title: Parliament panel seeks changes to Bill on removal of PM, CMs and Ministers after 30 days in jail
content: Draft report backs objective of preventing ‘governance from custody’ but recommends replacing ‘removal’ with ‘suspension’ and reworking arrest-based trigger; most Opposition parties had declined to join the panel arguing that the Bill was driven by ‘malicious intent’

title: Man accused in PoCSO case kills wife, 2 children and three more of complainant’s family in Telangana
content: According to the police, Rajkumar had been booked under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (PoCSO) Act following a complaint lodged by the minor girl and her mother in May, accusing him of sexual harassment.

title: CM Yogi claims Samajwadi Party, Congress allowed namaz at Ayodhya’s Hanumangarhi
content: Those pretending to have faith committed this sin, whereas the BJP established Ayodhya as the cultural capital of Sanatan Dharma, says Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath

title: Protests over Ken-Betwa river link project resume in Madhya Pradesh
content: Several protestors, most of them women, have launched a symbolic protest by placing hanging noose around their necks which has now reached its fifth day on the banks of the Barana River near Kupi village.

title: A decade of POCSO cases in Telangana: Over half remain pending trial as judicial backlog delays justice
content: Between January 1, 2016 and June 30, 2026, Telangana registered 31,117 cases under the POCSO Act. Of these, 16,348 cases, or 52.54%, remain pending trial, while another 2,863 cases (9.20%) are still under investigation.

title: BJP calls Congress’s charge against Union Home Minister on FCRA ‘baseless and politically motivated’
content: He said the Modi government did not operate on the “low-trust” policy of the Congress-led UPA government , but rather on “high accountability” and “nation first” principles.

title: 7,948 Assam schools without ideal pupil-teacher ratio
content: Education Minister Ranoj Pegu had earlier said that there are 2,670 single-teacher government primary schools

title: Frontal organisations backbone of Congress, says Dutt; issues directives for revamp in Haryana
content: Sanjay Dutt urged the organisation to focus on issues concerning women’s rights, women’s empowerment, increasing atrocities against women, inflation, unemployment and women’s safety.

title: 'One nation, one election' mechanism may be ready for rollout by 2029: Joint panel chief
content: P.P. Chaudhary claimed that nearly 99 % of civil society stakeholders consulted so far have backed the proposal

title: AAP to launch signature campaign for action in Ram Temple donation row
content: As part of the campaign, a letter addressed to Prime Minister Narendra Modi will be signed by people across India.

title: First batch of Kailash-Mansarovar Yatra pilgrims reaches Tibet
content: Officials from the Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam (KMVN) – the nodal agency for the yatra – stationed at the Dharchula base camp said the 48-member batch crossed the Lipulekh Pass at 9.05 a.m.

title: CPI questions PM Modi’s silence over Ram Temple case
content: Union government ‘betrayed trust of the people’, resignations alone would not suffice, says party general secretary Raja

title: Phone calls from 1600 number series cannot be blocked, DND app can block calls from 140 series: TRAI
content: The key objective of assigning designated series for these important communications is to make such calls trustworthy for the citizens, the regulator said

title: Civil rights organisations demand probe into Baruipur encounter
content: Representatives of civil rights group have raised questions over the police’s version of events leading to the encounter and sought an investigation by a judge

title: ₹3,800-crore assets seized in Mahadev betting app case: ED
content: The agency said its Raipur Zonal Office attached movable and immovable properties worth ₹941 crore belonging to businessman Vikas Garg and his family members

title: Man hurls papers at Supreme Court Bench after incoherent submissions
content: Litigant escorted out after disruption in court; court decides not to take action despite ‘unparliamentary utterances’

title: As National Conference canvasses for J&K Statehood protest, BJP announces stir
content: NC leader Tanvir Sadiq says all leaders should rise above party lines and stand united for the people of Jammu & Kashmir at the July 20 protest; BJP announces its own agenda with protests on employment issue on the same day

title: PM’s New Zealand visit will unlock new avenues of cooperation: govt.
content: During his two-day visit, PM Modi will meet with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and other leaders, focusing on enhancing economic and trade relations

title: NIA files charge sheet against six separatist leaders in 1996 Kashmir mob violence case
content: The six were charged under provisions of the Ranbir Penal Code, 1989, for criminal conspiracy, attempt to murder, rioting and assault on public servants, along with Section 13 of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967

title: Police freeze 12 more bank accounts of Trinamool with over ₹1,000 crore
content: Action follows complaints of illegal diversion of funds from the accounts; ED had earlier frozen party’s three accounts with ₹440 crore; High Court to take up case on Monday; Rajya Sabha MP slams ED action, asks when it will freeze temple funds

title: ‘Operation Toofan’: police step up vigil on drivers
content: According to police sources, the recent rise in cases involving synthetic drug use among some transport workers has raised concerns about passenger safety

title: Demolition of tilted building on M.G. Road deferred
content: The decision followed advice from expert engaged by the Corporation, who warned that demolition without precautionary measures could endanger nearby buildings and workers

title: Vijay announces memorial for Karur stampede victims, faults police for not informing him of the crowd
content: Speaking at a public meeting held on the outskirts of Karur, he says the tragedy could have been averted had the police alerted him to the gathering during his election campaign at Velusamypuram on September 27, 2025

title: Delhi's Red Fort Gets Bomb Threat, Turns Out To Be Hoax
content: The bomb threat comes nearly eight months after 15 people died and over a dozen were injured in a high-intensity vehicle-borne IED blast near the Red Fort Metro Station.

title: China's New Mega Dam Near Indian Border Poses Huge Geological Risk: Report
content: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.

title: Meta Discontinues AI Image Feature Days After Backlash Over Privacy Concerns
content: Meta, owner of Facebook and Instagram, had launched Muse Image on Tuesday, its first image-generation model from Meta Superintelligence Labs.

title: "1,000 Missiles Loaded": Trump Amid Iranians' Call For His Assassination
content: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after senior US officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open and that ships crossing the vital corridor won't be attacked any longer.

title: "J&K Integral Part Of India": Indian Diplomat Flags Wrong Map At Dhaka Event
content: The Indian diplomat, Pooja Kumari Jha, was attending a seminar at the Bangladesh Institute of International and Strategic Studies (BIISS) in Dhaka.

title: Two Kolkata deputy commissioners Suspended Over 2025 Remarks Against Suvendu Adhikari
content: The two officers suspended are Deputy Commissioner of Police III (North Division) Bidhan Saha and Deputy Commissioner of Police (Port Division II) Partha Pratim Das

title: Himachal, Uttarakhand Reel From Landslides, Mizoram, Tripura Battle Floods
content: In Himachal Pradesh, heavy to extremely heavy rains disrupted normal life in many parts, blocking roads, damaging bridges and uprooting trees. Schools remained closed in many parts of the state.

title: Bengal Man Poses As IAS Officer, Dupes Job Aspirant Of Rs 20 Lakh In Odisha
content: The accused identified as Rabinarayan Das, a native of Kendrapada district, is presently staying at Uttarpara police station area in Hooghly district of West Bengal.

title: Hyderabad Couple Goes Missing In Switzerland. They Took Crores From Investors
content: The missing couple, Pabba Chandrasekhar (51) - a businessman, and his wife, Swapna (42) - a homemaker, had left for their Switzerland holiday on June 22, their daughter, Shreya, said.

title: Martha Lillard, Last US Polio Patient Using Iron Lung, Dies At 78
content: Martha Lillard slept in the iron lung cylinder that encased her body as the air pressure in the chamber forced air in and out of her lungs.

title: PM Modi Receives Ceremonial Maori Welcome During New Zealand Visit
content: The ceremony featured a traditional Maori powhiri, a significant indigenous welcome that reflects New Zealand's rich cultural heritage and values.

title: PM Modi Praises India-New Zealand Trade Agreement As "Historic Milestone"
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the warmth shown by the people of New Zealand towards India was deeply moving and described the visit as a defining moment in bilateral ties.

title: Venezuela Earthquake Death Count Crosses 4,000, Thousands Still Missing
content: The stronger 7.5 magnitude quake -- the biggest in Venezuela in over a century -- struck 39 seconds after the first 7.2 magnitude shock, flattening entire high-rise apartment blocks to layers of rubble.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Reports Fresh American Strikes, Trump Updates Netanyahu
content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: Meta AI Image Detector Fails To Identify Some Of Its Own Cropped AI Pictures
content: In an analysis of 40 images generated using Muse Image, Reuters found the detection tool verified all of the original AI-generated images but failed to verify 55% of the same images after they were cropped.

title: Pak Forces Kills 75 Rebels In Balochistan After Cops Blindfolded, Shot Dead
content: The attackers abducted 18 police officers, who were later found blindfolded and shot dead after fleeing into nearby mountains.

title: 46 km In 45 Minutes: New Gurugram-Rewari Highway To Push Property Prices
content: Gurugram-Rewari Highway: Once operational, the highway is expected to cut travel time between Gurugram and Rewari to around 45 minutes.

title: Ex-UK MP Found Dead At Home With 'Serious Injuries', Murder Suspect Arrested
content: Ann Widdecombe, 78, was found dead on Thursday in her Haytor Vale home on the edge of Dartmoor National Park in southwest England after sustaining what police said were "serious injuries."

title: Restoration of memorial that gave Ranipet town its name nearing completion
content: Along with PWD and museum officials, Collector N. Priya inspected the restoration work of the memorial

title: NHAI plans to build underpasses at identified black spots in Tirupattur to prevent accidents
content: Highways officials said that on an average, five fatal accidents occur on each of the identified black spots on the stretch at regular intervals

title: Hit-and-run twist in fatal accident in Kerala capital; car driver nabbed based on CCTV evidence
content: Police apprehend car driver after establishing that the motorcycle was hit from behind before the four-wheeler sped away from the scene in Thiruvallam. Two young IT professionals were killed in the incident

title: HC order on TMC bank a/cs may not affect ED freeze
content: ED is not a party to the petition on which the HC gave its judgment . the freeze on three of 18 TMC bank accounts by ED under section 17 of PMLA was not the matter being decided by the agency . a HC order relaxing the restraint imposed by Kolkata police on all accounts .

title: Why do most wine bottles hold exactly 750 ml?
content: The TOI Lifestyle Desk is a dynamic team of dedicated journalists who, with unwavering passion and commitment, sift through the pulse of the nation to curate a vibrant tapestry of lifestyle news for The Times of India readers. At the TOI Lifestyle Desk, we go beyond the obvious, delving into the extraordinary. Consider us your lifestyle companion, providing a daily dose of inspiration and information. Whether you're seeking the latest fashion trends, travel escapades, culinary delights, or wellness tips, the TOI Lifestyle Desk is your one-stop destination for an enriching lifestyle experience. title: Cancer patient dead, her plea for drug listed 57 times for hearing
content: a petition for access to a life-saving breast cancer medicine has been listed for final hearing 57 times . the petitioner died in the early stage of the case, after which the HC decided to continue it suo motu . it is one of the most common cancers among women in India .

title: Modi, first PM in NZ in 40 years, to capitalise on FTA, boost ties
content: the two sides are expected to announce initiatives for collaboration in high-performance sports to boost India's chances of hosting the Olympic Games in 2036 . both countries are currently celebrating 100 years of sporting ties and looking to translate the centenary partnership into tangible outcomes for athletes, coaches and sporting ecosystems .

title: Trump dismantles poll body, raising suspicion of rigging
content: it is the first time in the 24-year history of the US Election Assistance Commission that all its commissioners have been removed simultaneously . democratic chairman Thomas Hicks and Democratic Commissioner Benjamin Hovland reportedly received termination notices from the White House Presidential Personnel Office . republican vice president Christy McCormick was allowed to resign .

title: Won't be feasible to offer pure petrol, lower blends as options to E20: Govt
content: govt ruled out offering pure petrol, lower ethanol-petrol blends as options to E20 . it also said E20 offers a higher-octane rating, superior anti-knock characteristics, faster combustion, better pickup, smoother acceleration and cleaner engine operation . the petroleum ministry said maintaining parallel nationwide supply chains would complicate distribution across more than one lakh retail outlets .
